source: single-www-reports: universal labels v1.0.3 (territories + slider scores aligned to live n8n prompt taxonomy as of 2026-05-15; skincare/cosmetics jargon removed from v1.0.2-www inherited from brand-alignment fork). Per-marker intro_heading, intro_description, category_*, condition_*, saturation_*, archetype_*, framework_attribution, two_axis_model, and extended_field_* are untouched.

changes: Align all 17 marker territory_N_title/_subtitle/_description to canonical taxonomy from live n8n workflow 52lZstOsQMhgspl8 system prompt 17markers_system_prompt (LIVE_2026-05-15 snapshot). 24 drifts resolved across markers 1-14, 17.
Critical fix: marker_5.territory_4_title 'The Luxury Icon' -> 'The Aspirational Authority' (data emits the latter; was a binding miss for any brand resolving to m5 t4).

Universalize slider_*_score_N labels to drop skincare/cosmetics residue (Pure Clinical, Lab/Natural Wisdom, Hydration, Pathology Cure, etc.) — replace with business-neutral equivalents that match the live prompt's universal vocabulary.

Out of scope (NOT changed in this version): intro_heading, intro_description, category_1..4, condition_*, saturation_*, marker_6 archetype_* + two_axis_model + framework_attribution, marker_8 extended_field_*. POKPOK X-RAY brand voice (vital sign, bedside manner, etc.) preserved deliberately.

REVERT v1.0.1: static_labels_www.json is the CANONICAL taxonomy (source of truth). Restored universal 'Component Purity' (marker_11) + 'The Components King' (marker_12). The n8n LLM prompt must be updated to reference this labels file so www_analysis.json territory_name values match this taxonomy, not the other way around.
Align marker_11 territory_2 back to 'Formula Purity' (www data uses this name)
Align marker_12 territory_1 back to 'The Ingredient King' (www data uses this name)
Revert over-aggressive 'ingredient' -> 'component' rewrite in KSP + USP markers
Forked from static_labels.json v1.4.1 for single-www-reports

Replaced category/the category-specific vocabulary (skin, components, performance features, derm, Protection, the specific issue, product, core product, category) with universal business vocabulary (customer, components, performance features, expert, protection, specific condition, product)
Removed specific brand name-drops (a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, a category leader, etc.)
Preserved marker_number, slider_*, territory_*, category_*, condition_* schema identically so Framer variable bindings resolve unchanged

binding_guide.markers: Access via labels.marker_1.marker_name, labels.marker_1.slider_a_title, labels.marker_1.territory_1_title, etc.

marker_1.intro_description: You have inputs (Mass) and marketing spend (Force). But without Purpose, you have no Direction. This isn't about 'saving the world'; it's the fundamental calculation the customer's brain makes in milliseconds: 'Does this brand exist to fix my problem, protect me, connect with me, or help me become more?' The website must answer this in the first scroll, or the customer moves on.
marker_1.slider_a_title: [SLIDER] ORIGIN OF AUTHORITY (The Source of Power)
marker_1.slider_a_score_3: ▼ Hybrid Evidence (Technology refined by human insight)
marker_1.slider_a_score_5: ▼ Pure Human Insight (Lived experience, founder vision)
marker_1.territory_1_description: Problems not preferences. Proof code: audits, diagnostics, technical intervention, measurable repair.
marker_1.territory_2_description: Shields against the modern world. Immunity, protection, barrier defense. Prevention over reaction.
marker_1.territory_3_description: Restores equilibrium through fit, clarity, restraint, and human logic. Gentleness over force.
marker_1.territory_4_description: Treats motivation, confidence, and identity — not just the surface problem. The payoff is psychological unlock, not only functional improvement.

marker_2.intro_description: Most brands treat Mission as a poetic slogan. Wrong. On the website, Mission is a targeting system. It defines the Blast Radius of your ambition. The customer subconsciously calculates this instantly: 'Is this brand trying to fix a narrow problem, or is it trying to reshape an entire category?' 'Is this club for the elite few, or the democratic many?' If the website doesn't define the size of the battlefield, you aren't a leader. You are a bystander.
marker_2.slider_b_score_1: ▼ The Velvet Rope (Gatekept by price or license)
marker_2.slider_b_score_4: ▼ Approachable (Easy to find, easy to buy)
marker_2.territory_1_description: Accessible value. Simple inputs, ruthlessly efficient. Not 'cheap'; smart.
marker_2.territory_2_description: Elite specialist capability. Deep expertise, advanced methods, proprietary systems, research, or technical authority. If you ask the price, you are not the core buyer.
marker_2.territory_3_description: Broad accessible mission. The offer is a vehicle for improving a market, community, category, or ecosystem. Buy into shared progress.
marker_2.territory_4_description: Elite global mission. A manifesto that requires 'education' to decode. Selective, not universal.

marker_3.intro_description: Values aren't inspirational statements on a website. On the website, they are the Operating Code that resolves conflict. When 'High Potency' clashes with 'Zero Harm,' which one wins? When 'Marketing Magic' clashes with 'Radical Transparency,' which one wins? Values show themselves in the trade-offs the brand refuses to make — they are visible in how the website presents its components, guarantees, and proofs.
marker_3.slider_b_score_1: ▼ The Spec Sheet (Exact percentages, technical specifications, sourcing)
marker_3.slider_b_score_5: ▼ The Black Box (Trust our proprietary system)
marker_3.territory_1_description: Code: 'Do No Harm.' Sacrifices maximum force for reliability, trust, accessibility, and low-risk adoption. 'Expert-tested,' audited, compliant, verified, transparent.
marker_3.territory_2_description: Code: 'Show the Receipts.' Sacrifices mystery for proof. Percentages, technical studies, full specifications.
marker_3.territory_3_description: Code: 'Respect the Source.' Sacrifices data density for legacy, craft, ritual, origin story, founder philosophy, or cultural continuity.
marker_3.territory_4_description: Code: 'Results at All Costs.' Proprietary method, protected IP, high-performance system, or aspirational outcome claims.

marker_4.intro_description: Positioning isn't a slide in a pitch deck; it is the specific parking spot you occupy in the customer's brain. The brain hates chaos, so it simplifies brands into archetypes to save energy. On the website, you are either the Expert (trusted), the Specialist (advanced), the Staple (reliable), or the Essential (trendy). Everything on the website — from layout to photography to language — is reinforcing exactly one of these slots.
marker_4.territory_1_description: Functional × Utility. SLOT: BANK / LAB / GOV / INFRASTRUCTURE. Boring on purpose. Trust before emotion.
marker_4.territory_2_description: Functional × Prestige. SLOT: ENTERPRISE / EXPERT / PREMIUM PLATFORM. Technical codes justify premium.
marker_4.territory_3_description: Lifestyle × Utility. SLOT: MASS / COMMUNITY / BROAD ACCESS. Warm, easy, safe, and broadly useful. 'For everyone,' 'everyday,' 'simple,' or low-friction cues.
marker_4.territory_4_description: Expressive × Prestige. SLOT: DESIGN-LED / PREMIUM / CULTURAL. Minimal, curated, distinctive, high-taste.

marker_5.intro_description: If Archetype is the Role you play, Personality is how you behave in the room. Based on Jennifer Aaker's Dimensions, we measure the behavioral traits the website projects. Does the brand act like a distant, clinical authority (High Competence)? Or like a trusted friend (High Sincerity)? The personality is encoded in every word, image, and interaction on the website.
marker_5.territory_1_description: High Warmth (4-5), Low Authority (1). Honest, caring, down-to-earth. Safe, family-friendly.
marker_5.territory_2_description: High Warmth (4-5), Low-Mid Authority (2-3). Cool, energetic, contemporary. Viral, fun.
marker_5.territory_3_description: Low Warmth (1-2), High Authority (4-5). Restrained, precise, in control. Effective, serious.
marker_5.territory_4_description: Mid Warmth (3-4), High Authority (4-5). Polished, composed, curated, and status-aware. The brand feels desirable because it knows more, looks sharper, and carries premium social weight.

marker_6.framework_attribution: Based on the Pearson-Marr Archetype Indicator® (PMAI®) system developed by Dr. Carol S. Pearson and Margaret Mark. https://www.carolspearson.com/about/archetypal-assessment-pearson-marr-archetype-indicator-r
marker_6.intro_description: Archetypes aren't tarot cards; they are efficiency hacks for the human brain. The brain is lazy. It wants to categorize you instantly to save energy. 'Is this brand the Teacher (Sage) or the Rebel (Outlaw)?' On the website, your archetype mix determines the story you tell, the language you use, and the community you attract. We measure the dominant primary archetype plus two supporting influences.
marker_6.archetype_group_a_description: Care-focused. Safe, expert-endorsed, 'everyone belongs' inclusivity. Stability through community. Offers certainty in a chaotic world. The website signals protection, reliability, and structure. Safe, nurturing, and welcoming to all.
marker_6.archetype_group_b_description: Results-obsessed. High potency, visible transformation, 'this changes your reality' energy. Zero hand-holding. Exists to overthrow the status quo. The website signals intensity, rule-breaking, and transformation. Results at any cost.
marker_6.archetype_group_c_description: Community-powered. Cult following, influencer-led, 'join the movement' vibe. Mastery through belonging. Prioritizes the tribe over the product. The website signals warmth, inclusivity, and sensory fun. It feels like a club.
marker_6.archetype_group_d_description: Truth-seeking. Minimalist, transparent, evidence-led. 'Understand the truth' approach. Driven by the search for answers. The website signals purity, simplicity, and raw data. A return to the source.
marker_6.archetype_caregiver_description: The Altruist. Driven to protect and nurture others.
marker_6.archetype_ruler_description: The Boss. Driven to create order and prosperity.
marker_6.archetype_creator_description: The Architect. Driven to imagine and build the new.
marker_6.archetype_sage_description: The Scholar. Driven to understand the world through data.
marker_6.archetype_innocent_description: The Idealist. Driven by purity, simplicity, and faith.
marker_6.archetype_explorer_description: The Seeker. Driven by autonomy and new experiences.
marker_6.archetype_hero_description: The Warrior. Driven to prove worth through action.
marker_6.archetype_magician_description: The Visionary. Driven to turn dreams into reality.
marker_6.archetype_outlaw_description: The Rebel. Driven to break rules that don't work.
marker_6.archetype_everyman_description: The Neighbor. Driven to fit in and connect.
marker_6.archetype_jester_description: The Entertainer. Driven to live in the moment.
marker_6.archetype_lover_description: The Partner. Driven by desire and connection.
marker_6.territory_1_description: Results-obsessed. Visible transformation, bold promises backed by measurable outcomes. Proven claims, data-first.
marker_6.territory_2_description: Community-powered. Cult following, influencer-led. Mastery through belonging. Loved by category enthusiasts. UGC and tribe language dominate.
marker_6.territory_3_description: Truth-seeking. Minimalist, transparent, evidence-driven. Research-led explanations. Zero marketing fluff. Returns to the source.
marker_6.territory_4_description: Care-focused. Safe, expert-endorsed. Stability through community. Reliable for the most cautious users. Expert quotes. Emphasizes protection and universal belonging.

marker_7.intro_description: Tone of Voice isn't about what you say; it is the frequency you broadcast on. On the website, the customer isn't just reading; they are listening to the vibe. Are you a lecturer or a drinking buddy? A scientist or a cheerleader? We measure tone across four axes: Humor, Formality, Respect, and Enthusiasm — because each axis signals a different relationship with the reader.
marker_7.slider_a_score_3: ▼ Light (Pleasant, not trying to be funny)
marker_7.territory_1_description: Academic & dry. Audit/report voice. H≤1-2, E≤1-2, F≤1-3, R≤1-3. Serious, competent.
marker_7.territory_2_description: Direct & concise. Instruction manual by a human. H≤1-3, E≤2-3, F≤2-4, R≤2-3. Clear, efficient.
marker_7.territory_3_description: Warm & helpful. Good neighbor. E≤3-4, F≤2-4, H≤2-3, R≤2-3. Friendly, supportive.
marker_7.territory_4_description: Loud & playful. Text thread energy. E≤4-5 AND (F≤4-5 OR H≤4-5 OR R≤4-5). Fun, bold.

marker_8.intro_description: Your customer processes visuals 60,000 times faster than text. Before they read a word, their brain has already decoded your Vibe (Safe vs. Fun) and looked for your Anchors (Codes). We measure this in two layers: First, the Visual Temperature (Cold / Warm). Second, the Layout Entropy (Minimalist / Expressive). The combined signal tells the brain who you are before any copy is read.
marker_8.territory_1_description: Cool, restrained, structured visual language. Uses white space, grid logic, technical cues, institutional restraint, and low-risk clarity to signal competence and authority.
marker_8.territory_2_description: Warm, saturated, high-expression visual language. Uses bold color, motion, stickers, illustration, social-native codes, playful composition, or high-energy cues to signal fun, immediacy, and cultural fluency.
marker_8.territory_3_description: Warm, expressive, or emotionally charged visuals contained inside a clear system. Human energy without chaos. Works through inviting color, people, texture, community, photography, illustration, or lived-world cues, while keeping hierarchy and control.
marker_8.territory_4_description: Cool or severe visual codes combined with expressive layout tension. Experimental structure, stark contrast, abstraction, asymmetry, or deliberate difficulty signal rebellion, cultural edge, or category challenge.

marker_9.intro_description: Messaging isn't about writing long paragraphs; it's about the Hook. On the website, the customer reads your Headline and maybe one sub-header. That's it. In those 2 seconds, you either hook them with a Component (Logic), a Feature (Action), a Result (Outcome), or a Dream (Identity). Pick wrong, and the audience is gone.
marker_9.territory_1_description: Sells the components. Breaks the offer into mechanisms, modules, features, steps, or proof points. Rational, structured, detail-led.
marker_9.territory_2_description: Sells the relief. Pain point → practical fix. Clear utility, direct outcome, low theater.
marker_9.territory_3_description: Sells the future state. Focuses on aspiration, transformation, status, or emotional payoff more than mechanics.
marker_9.territory_4_description: Sells the source of belief. Elevates origin, philosophy, worldview, craft, culture, or mission into the reason to care.

marker_10.intro_description: A product needs a factory; a brand needs a birth certificate. On the website, the 'Story' isn't a fairy tale; it is the Trust Anchor. It answers the question: 'Who birthed this, and why?' Did an expert create it from rigorous research, or did a survivor create it from personal struggle? The protagonist and setting of this story encode what kind of brand you actually are.
marker_10.slider_a_score_5: ▼ The Survivor (Founder who solved own problem)
marker_10.territory_1_description: Born from research. The story is about time, expertise, and rigor. 'We studied this for 10 years.' Trust is built on data.
marker_10.territory_2_description: Born from personal experience. Founder had the problem, created the solution through trial and method. Trust is built on shared pain + credibility.
marker_10.territory_3_description: Born from real-world insight, validated by method. Experts extract and validate sources using rigorous methods. Trust is built on validated authenticity.
marker_10.territory_4_description: Born from wisdom. Founder rediscovers an ancient practice or source. Trust is built on heritage and personal journey.

marker_11.intro_description: Claims are cheap; proof is currency. On the website, the customer is a skeptic. They are looking for the 'Reason to Believe' (RTB). KSPs are the bullets you fire to kill that skepticism. We measure this because your proof source — expert authority, peer validation, purity certifications, or viral results — signals exactly what type of buyer you are courting.
marker_11.slider_a_score_3: ▼ The Certifiers (Third-party certifications, compliance bodies, industry logos)
marker_11.slider_a_score_4: ▼ The Users (Percentage of users who agree)
marker_11.slider_b_score_5: ▼ The Feel (Focus on experience, ease, flow)
marker_11.territory_1_description: Expert proof. Studies, benchmarks, testimonials, advisors, institutions, or specialist recommendations.
marker_11.territory_2_description: Checklist proof. Certifications, standards, features, integrations, compliance, or attribute lists.
marker_11.territory_3_description: Crowd proof. Demos, examples, usage proof, community proof, rankings, reviews, or user statistics.
marker_11.territory_4_description: Experience proof. Ease, clarity, flow, satisfaction, emotional response, or user-experience descriptions.

marker_12.marker_name: USP (A Unique Selling Point or Proposition)
marker_12.intro_description: The USP is the sharpest knife in the drawer. It is not a list of features; it is the one thing you own that no one else can steal. On the website, the customer's brain tries to pigeonhole you instantly. We measure the flavor of your ownership claim (Component story vs. Technology story) and the scope of your target (Mass vs. Niche). The combination defines whether you win by depth or by reach.
marker_12.slider_a_title: [SLIDER] THE OWNERSHIP CLAIM (What Makes You Unique)
marker_12.slider_a_score_2: ▼ Hero Feature (Owned mental link: 'We are the [category leader]')
marker_12.slider_b_title: [SLIDER] THE TARGET SCOPE (How Wide Is The Net?)
marker_12.territory_1_description: Hero Input/Component (A=2) × Mid-Market (B=3). EDGE: SIGNATURE ELEMENT. Owns the mental link to one distinctive input, capability, feature, source, or category cue. Trust comes from consistency and repeated ownership of that element.
marker_12.territory_2_description: Delivery System (A=4-5) × Mid-Market (B=3). EDGE: PERFORMANCE. How it works, not only what it contains. Mechanism, infrastructure, automation, process, or engineered superiority.
marker_12.territory_3_description: Complex/Tech (A=2-4) × Price Leader (B=1-2). EDGE: EFFICIENCY. Advanced capability at accessible prices. Radical transparency + unbeatable value.
marker_12.territory_4_description: Any (A) × Niche (B=4-5). EDGE: DIAGNOSIS. Hyper-targeted to one problem, role, market, or use case. Specialist reputation within a single problem space.

marker_13.intro_description: Messaging is what you say; Focus is what you do. On the website, you have limited pixels. How you spend them reveals your true priority. Are you allocating space to 80% diagrams (Education) or 80% atmospheric imagery (Lifestyle)? We measure two axes: Depth (Education vs. Inspiration) and Intent (Trust vs. Commerce). The ratio signals what the brand prioritizes.
marker_13.territory_1_description: Classroom. Frameworks, diagrams, evidence, proof, and hard logic.
marker_13.territory_2_description: Community board. Testimonials, user proof, case studies, logos, rankings, and crowd trust.
marker_13.territory_3_description: Mood board. Lifestyle, aspiration, identity, aesthetic desire, and emotional pull.
marker_13.territory_4_description: Offer page. Plans, bundles, CTAs, forms, discounts, demos, and transaction speed.

marker_14.intro_description: This is the 'Job to be Done.' Customers don't buy features; they buy a result. On the website, the promise is binary: either you are Stopping Damage (Prevention) or Fixing Damage (Restoration). Either you are improving Performance (Function) or improving Appearance (Aesthetic). We measure these two vectors because they determine whether your buyer comes for pain relief or for aspiration.
marker_14.slider_a_score_5: ▼ The Fixer (Reverses damage, problems, visible issues)
marker_14.territory_1_description: Reduces uncertainty, error, exposure, confusion, operational pain, or reputational risk. Urgent recovery or reassurance.
marker_14.territory_2_description: Protects assets, trust, time, attention, privacy, quality, continuity, or control. Total defense.
marker_14.territory_3_description: Saves time, simplifies work, improves flow, reduces friction, and makes action easier. Useful operational balance.
marker_14.territory_4_description: Gives the user, team, buyer, or community new ability, knowledge, access, status, performance, or strategic leverage.

marker_15.intro_description: Functional is what the product does to the world; Emotional is what it does to the mind. On the website, we measure the 'Psychological Payoff.' Are you selling the absence of pain (Relief) or the presence of joy (Reward)? Are you selling inner safety (Sanctuary) or outer status (Signal)? This is the real currency of the purchase.
marker_15.slider_a_score_1: ▼ Panic Mode (Urgent need to stop pain or risk)
marker_15.slider_a_score_3: ▼ Contentment (The feeling of a job well done)
marker_15.slider_b_score_3: ▼ Readiness (I am ready to face the day)
marker_15.slider_b_score_4: ▼ Savvy (I feel smart/validated for choosing this)
marker_15.territory_1_description: The absence of worry. 'I don't have to stress about this decision.' It sells peace of mind and security.
marker_15.territory_2_description: The removal of insecurity. 'I can face the world without hiding.' It sells the ability to be seen.
marker_15.territory_3_description: The presence of joy. 'I deserve this moment.' It sells a dopamine hit and a private moment of luxury.
marker_15.territory_4_description: The feeling of being smart. 'I didn't fall for the marketing; I bought the smart choice.' It sells savvy.

marker_16.intro_description: This isn't about what the product does for you; it's about what the product says about you. Every purchase is a vote for a specific identity. When a customer puts a product on their desk, they are signaling a value system to everyone who enters the room. The website either makes this signal loud or leaves it ambiguous.
marker_16.slider_a_score_2: ▼ The Smart Choice (because it makes sense)
marker_16.slider_a_score_3: ▼ The Balance (It works well and looks good)
marker_16.slider_a_score_4: ▼ The Statement (I buy it to show my taste)
marker_16.slider_a_score_5: ▼ The Avatar (I buy it to define who I am)
marker_16.slider_b_score_1: ▼ The Standard (What everyone uses; the default)
marker_16.slider_b_score_4: ▼ The Insider (Hard to find; "if you know, you know")
marker_16.slider_b_score_5: ▼ The Exclusive (Gatekept by price or access)
marker_16.territory_1_description: "I don't fall for marketing fluff; I buy what works." Signals intelligence and immunity to hype. The badge of the realist.
marker_16.territory_2_description: "I know what is cool right now." Signals social awareness and youth. The badge of the person who is plugged in.
marker_16.territory_3_description: "I care about the details." Signals moral superiority or deep knowledge. The badge of the ethical or educated buyer.
marker_16.territory_4_description: "I can afford the absolute best." Signals financial power and taste. The badge of the elite.

marker_17.intro_description: Humans are herd animals. We don't just buy products to use them; we buy them to signal where we belong in the hierarchy. On the website, the brand offers you a membership card. It asks: 'Do you want to join the Majority (Safety in numbers), the Knowledge Club (Intellectual tribe), the Ethical Warriors (Moral tribe), or the Insider Clique (Elite few)?'
marker_17.slider_a_score_1: ▼ Normalization ("I want to look like everyone else")
marker_17.slider_a_score_2: ▼ Acceptance ("I want to be accepted by the group")
marker_17.slider_a_score_5: ▼ Separation ("I want to be unlike the rest")
marker_17.territory_1_description: Safety in numbers. '#1 bestseller,' 'trusted by millions.' Normal, sensible.
marker_17.territory_2_description: 'In the know.' Component jargon, expert blogs, customer-context insider buzz.
marker_17.territory_3_description: Moral action. Compliant, ethical, sustainable, climate claims, activism language.
marker_17.territory_4_description: Access + taste. Limited drops, invite-only, subtle codes for insiders.
